macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise on Iran Ceasefire Hopes as Oil Prices Ease
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures gained Tuesday as mediation efforts in the Middle East lifted sentiment and pushed oil prices lower, with Brent crude retreating from a one-month high near $90 per barrel.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller Signals Potential Rate Hike if Inflation Disappoints
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller stated Monday that a hot inflation reading this week could necessitate a near-term interest rate hike, keeping rate expectations elevated.

items.title: Trump Poised to Unleash Fresh Tariffs on Dozens of Countries This Week
items.summary: President Donald Trump is expected to impose new tariffs on multiple nations as his temporary 10% global tariff expires Friday, with the Financial Times reporting additional 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods already imposed.

items.title: Major Companies Beat Earnings Expectations: GM, Micron, Northrop Grumman, and 3M
items.summary: General Motors, Micron Technology, Northrop Grumman, and 3M all reported earnings above expectations, with GM posting $3.57 per share and Northrop Grumman at $7.68 per share.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Jump to Highest Level of 2026, Blowing Blow to Housing Market
items.summary: Mortgage rates edged up to the highest level in a year, delivering fresh pressure to the U.S. housing market amid persistent inflation concerns.

items.title: Apple Regains World's Most Valuable Company Spot, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple reclaimed the top global valuation at $4.88 trillion after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, reversing a recent leadership shift in the AI sector.

items.title: Inflation Slowed to 3.5% in June as Gasoline Prices Fell
items.summary: U.S. inflation eased to 3.5% in June, providing consumers a break from high gasoline costs, though wholesale prices fell for the first time in 10 months largely due to lower gas prices.

items.title: Euro Zone Banks Tighten Credit Access Amid Geopolitical Instability Fears
items.summary: The European Central Bank's quarterly survey revealed that Euro zone banks tightened credit access in Q2 due to geopolitical instability, with expectations for further tightening in the current quarter.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin surges past $66K as crypto market adds $70B in daily value
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed to a monthly peak of $66,300, up over 3% in 24 hours, while the total crypto market cap rose $70B to $2.32T amid optimism over the CLARITY Act and renewed institutional inflows into Bitcoin ETFs.

items.title: Cardano activates Van Rossem hard fork to reduce smart contract costs
items.summary: Cardano developers successfully activated the Van Rossem hard fork on mainnet, designed to lower Plutus smart contract execution costs and prepare the network for future upgrades.

items.title: SEC charges Florida man and company in $22M crypto mining fraud
items.summary: The SEC filed charges against a Florida individual and company for allegedly orchestrating a $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme, marking another major enforcement action in the sector.

items.title: Washington judge blocks Kalshi's sports prediction markets over state gambling laws
items.summary: A federal judge in Washington blocked Kalshi from operating sports prediction markets, citing conflicts with state gambling laws, while Polymarket and Kalshi open interest fell 20% as World Cup frenzy subsided.

items.title: Grayscale files ETF for Worldcoin, could list on Wall Street
items.summary: Grayscale submitted an ETF filing for Worldcoin with the SEC, potentially enabling the project to list on Wall Street and expand institutional access to the AI-identity token.

items.title: Dogecoin plunges 23% amid US-Iran geopolitical tensions
items.summary: Dogecoin suffered a steep 23.1% decline to $0.072 on July 20 as escalating US-Iran hostilities triggered risk aversion in crypto markets, while Bitcoin traded near $64,700 under modest pressure.

items.title: Former NY Governor Cuomo joins OKX board, promotes tokenized trading
items.summary: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo joined OKX's board of directors, highlighting the exchange's 24/7 tokenized trading platform and expanding its regulatory credibility.

items.title: Vietnam imposes crypto trading fines matching drunk-driving penalties
items.summary: Vietnam introduced new cryptocurrency trading fines equivalent to drunk-driving penalties, signaling stricter enforcement and higher costs for illegal crypto activities.

items.title: Russian parliament passes law regulating crypto market
items.summary: Russia's parliament passed a comprehensive law regulating the cryptocurrency market, establishing legal frameworks for digital asset operations and compliance within the country.

items.title: BitMine adds 7,430 ETH, now holds 5.78M ETH (4.8% of supply)
items.summary: BitMine purchased an additional 7,430 ETH last week, bringing its total Ethereum reserves to 5.78 million ETH, representing over 4.8% of the total ETH supply, and repurchased $4B in shares.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight Model at 2.8 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3 today, a 2.8-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with a 1-million-token context window and native vision, temporarily pausing new subscriptions due to overwhelming demand that strained capacity[8][6].

items.title: Anthropic's $1.5B Copyright Settlement with Authors Approved by Federal Judge
items.summary: A federal judge in San Francisco signed off on Anthropic's landmark $1.5 billion settlement resolving a class-action lawsuit where authors accused the company of misusing their books to train its Claude chatbot[1][2].

items.title: Google Developing 'Frozen v2' Server Chip Embedding Gemini Model Directly into Silicon
items.summary: Google is developing a new server chip called 'Frozen v2' that permanently etches part of its Gemini AI architecture into silicon to skip decisions and reduce data movement, projected to be 6–10 times more efficient than current TPUs by 2028[1][5].

items.title: NVIDIA Announces Cosmos 3 Edge and Synthetic Video Detector at SIGGRAPH 2026
items.summary: At SIGGRAPH 2026, NVIDIA unveiled Cosmos 3 Edge, a billion-parameter open world model for real-time physical AI on local devices, and a Synthetic Video Detector microservice to identify AI-generated video frame-by-frame[5].

items.title: Microsoft Becomes First Public Customer for AMD's Helios Rack-Scale AI Platform
items.summary: Microsoft deployed AMD's Helios platform featuring the 72-GPU MI455X system with 31.1TB HBM4 memory on Azure to power frontier-model inference, marking the first publicly named customer for the rack-scale AI system[8].

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red Automated Red-Teaming Model to Detect Prompt Injections
items.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming model trained via self-play reinforcement learning specifically to find and exploit prompt injection vulnerabilities in AI systems[5].

items.title: Sakana AI Releases Fugu-Cyber Multi-Agent Model for Enterprise Cybersecurity
items.summary: Sakana AI released Fugu-Cyber, a multi-agent orchestration model purpose-built for enterprise cybersecurity, now available as a new API endpoint[5].

items.title: US and China Plan September AI Talks to Address Frontier Model Regulation Risks
items.summary: Five sources familiar with the matter told Reuters that the United States and China are planning to hold talks over AI in September as superpowers confront how to regulate risks from increasingly powerful rival frontier models[1].

items.title: New York Bans Data Center Construction for One Year, Rattling AI Industry
items.summary: New York imposed a one-year moratorium on data center construction, which may become a blueprint for the emerging anti-AI movement and is causing significant concern in the AI infrastructure sector[10].

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft by Ex-Engineer
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI accusing the company of conspiring with former Apple employees who allegedly used a bug to steal trade secrets[10].

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: The Witcher 3: Songs of the Past Reveal Date Confirmed for Gamescom 2026
items.summary: CD Projekt announced that The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt — Songs of the Past will be revealed at gamescom 2026 during Opening Night Live, marking a major expansion reveal for the franchise.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Beta and NEXT Showcase Announced
items.summary: Activision confirmed the Call of Duty: NEXT showcase on August 21st will debut live gameplay for Modern Warfare 4, though Nintendo Switch 2 will miss the first week of the beta.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms Roadmap Including 4 Fallout Games and Elder Scrolls 6
items.summary: Bethesda revealed a development roadmap featuring four new Fallout games, Elder Scrolls 6, and Creation Engine 3, while also confirming Fallout 3 and New Vegas remasters are in development.

items.title: Microsoft Layoffs Hit Bethesda and id Software Hard
items.summary: Reports indicate Microsoft layoffs have affected up to 50% of some teams at Bethesda and id Software, with further reductions potentially coming amid ongoing industry restructuring.

items.title: Persona 4 Revival Remake Confirmed for February 2027 Release
items.summary: ATLUS released a new Persona 4 Revival trailer spotlighting Chie Satonaka, confirming the remake's release date as February 18, 2027, for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC.

items.title: UK Game Store GAME Falls to Administration with £16M Debt
items.summary: Major UK video game retailer GAME has entered administration due to £16 million in debt, signaling significant instability in the European physical retail sector.

items.title: Final Fantasy X/X-2 HD Remaster Coming to Switch 2 This Week
items.summary: A 25th anniversary trailer for Final Fantasy X was released ahead of the HD Remaster's launch on Switch 2, though Switch owners cannot update to the Switch 2 port.

items.title: Saudi PIF Acquisition of Electronic Arts Set for EU Approval
items.summary: The European Union is reportedly set to approve Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund acquisition of Electronic Arts, marking a major shift in gaming industry ownership.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple dethrones Nvidia as world's most valuable company
items.summary: Apple has ended Nvidia's long run at the top, becoming the world's most valuable company in a major shift in tech industry leadership.

items.title: New York imposes first statewide moratorium on data centers
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers that fuel AI for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium.

items.title: Samsung plans August beta launch of AI-powered Health Assistant
items.summary: Samsung Electronics is rolling out a new AI-powered Samsung Health Assistant with new heart metrics tools like Vitals for overnight tracking in beta in August.

items.title: Judge approves Anthropic's $1.5B settlement in AI copyright lawsuit
items.summary: A federal judge in San Francisco approved Anthropic's landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a class action lawsuit brought by authors who accused the company of misusing their books to train its AI chatbot.

items.title: X rebuilds Android app entirely in major engineering project
items.summary: X has completed a significant engineering project by rebuilding its Android application entirely, promising a faster and more reliable user experience for its platform.

items.title: Paramount-Warner Bros Discovery merger temporarily suspended
items.summary: A federal court has intervened to temporarily suspend the merger of Paramount and Warner Bros Discovery following objections from twelve states concerned about film industry competitiveness.

items.title: IQE raises annual sales growth forecast on data center demand
items.summary: UK semiconductor wafer maker IQE upgraded its 2026 revenue growth forecast to above 30% from 20% previously, driven by rising demand for semiconductor products used in data centers.

items.title: Ant International raises $1.2 billion for global expansion
items.summary: Ant International has raised $1.2 billion in a global expansion push, strengthening its position in the digital payments and tech sector.
